description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a dual-wing structure, featuring a wooden and fabric-covered frame with visible struts and rigging wires connecting the upper and lower wings. The fuselage is slender and streamlined, with a forward-mounted open cockpit and a prominent wooden propeller at the nose. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The tail section includes twin vertical stabilizers and horizontal stabilizers, each adorned with roundel insignia and colored stripes. The wings are straight and parallel, with no visible sweep or dihedral, and display roundel markings on the upper surfaces. The overall color scheme is a combination of tan, olive green, and red-white-blue accents, evoking early 20th-century military aircraft.
description: A vintage biplane with dual straight wings, open cockpit, wooden propeller, and twin vertical stabilizers, finished in tan and olive with roundel markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual wings, open cockpit, wooden propeller, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the straight biplane wings, roundel markings, and twin tail stabilizers.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the twin vertical stabilizers, tail skid, and the structure of the tail section.
description_view_3: Bottom-up rear angle showing the undercarriage, lower wing, and tail assembly.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single forward-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and a single horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted open cockpit, likely single or tandem seat, with no enclosed canopy.
